old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-vyoy","title":"[spec-compliance] RPL Trickle Imin constant mismatch (4096 vs 4000ms)","description":"constants.toml:59 sets imin_ms=4096 and python/src/lichen/constants.py:52 uses RPL_TRICKLE_IMIN_MS=4096. However, spec/drafts/draft-lichen-rpl-lora-00.md:235 explicitly states \"Imin = 4 s = 4000 ms\" and Imax calculation based on that. The C example in implementation status also uses 4*1000. This is a wrong constant differing from spec (MUST match normative values). Reviewed in commit 3f429277a which updated RPL section but left mismatch. Also check Rust/C impls for used value.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T03:13:43.895086238Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T03:13:43.895086238Z"}

new_value: Updated constants.toml, python constants.py, and Kconfig default to 4000ms to match spec Imin=4000ms. Updated comments. Rust uses DIO config exponent (12 for ~4s). Tests unaffected.
